sections.paragraph:
ἐκφεύγω fut. ἐκφεύξομαι; 2 aor. ἐξέφυγον; 2 pf. ἐκπέφευγα (s. φεύγω; Hom. et al.; pap, LXX; En 10:3, 17; PsSol 15:8; TestAbr A 8 p. 85, 28 [Stone p. 18]; GrBar 7:5; Just.).

① to seek safety in flight, run away (2 Km 17:2 A) Ac 16:27; ἐκ τ. οἴκου Ac 19:16.

② to become free from danger by avoiding some peril, escape

ⓐ abs. (Hdt. 5, 95, 1 φεύγων ἐκφεύγει=he escapes by flight; Lucian, Alex. 28; ViEzk 10 [p. 75, 3 Sch.]; Jos., Bell. 1, 65, Ant. 2, 341) fig. (Sir 16:13) 1 Th 5:3; Hb 2:3; 12:25.

ⓑ w. acc. of that which one escapes

α. lit., a monster (Artem. 4 p. 200, 25 θηρίον ἐ.) Hv 4, 2, 3.

β. fig. (Diod S 5, 59, 2 τὸ μύσος ἐ.; 17, 112, 3 τὸν κίνδυνον; Ael. Aristid. 34 p. 656 D.: τὴν αἰσχύνην; EpArist 268; Philo, Leg. All. 3, 93 λήθην) tribulations Lk 21:36; Hv 4, 2, 4b and 5; judgment (κρίσιν: 2 Macc 7:35; Just., A I, 68, 2 al.; cp. PRyl 28, 164ff ἐν κρίσει βαρηθεὶς ἔσται καὶ ἐκφεύξεται) τὸ κρίμα τοῦ θεοῦ Ro 2:3. τὰς χεῖράς τινος (Phalaris, Ep. 92; Sus 22; Tob 13:2) 2 Cor 11:33. τὸ ἀποθανεῖν (Pla., Apol. 39a) ITr 2:1.

ⓒ καλῶς ἐξέφυγες you have made good your escape or you deserved to escape Hv 4, 2, 4a.

③ to be aware of a peril and take measures to avoid it, shun, avoid τὶ (Epict. 4, 5, 2 Σωκράτης πανταχοῦ ἐξέφυγεν μάχην; Diog. L. 10, 117; Philo, Leg. All. 3, 236 κακίαν) the world Hv 4, 3, 4.—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

abs. abs. = absolute

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

ViEzk ViEzk , s. ViIs

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

acc. acc. = accusative

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 312
